hypothesis: IB-T010 E2b is the pre-declared follow-up to E2's honest REFUTED verdict (docs/evidence/ib-t010/benchmark-report-1.md Sec 3, +25.16% accepted-TTFT p95 degradation at 5x, root-caused to queue-transit: at 5x offered rate the shallow bounded per-tenant/global queue (cap=3) is perpetually full, so nearly every accepted request carries one full-queue transit, ~+67ms at p50). Shrinking the queue cap from 3 to 1 (both -admission-tenant-queue-cap and -admission-global-queue-cap move together -- see 'variable' below) is expected to cut that queue-transit contribution roughly 3x toward the off-arm's ~83ms no-queue floor (E2 admission-off-v1 5x accepted p50 82.2ms), pulling accepted-request TTFT p95 degradation at ~5x offered rate (vs the SAME-config ~1x capacity-boundary baseline) under the G5 <=20% criterion. The expected trade is a HIGHER shed rate than E2's sane-v1 config at the same offered rate (less queueing headroom means more early sheds), with sheds remaining typed 503 overloaded + Retry-After (unchanged mechanism, IG-T010) -- this trade is reported adjacent to every latency table, never hidden behind the goodput ratio, per experiments.md rule 7. If E2b ALSO fails the <=20% criterion, that is a second failure of the same underlying admission-value hypothesis for the same root cause (queue-transit at 5x) and this task does NOT iterate to a third config (e.g. cap=0, which is degenerate: it sheds everything with zero queueing and is out of scope by construction) -- it records the result and stops, per the fresh-context G5 gate verifier's explicit prescription.
expected_direction: admission-sane-v1b (queue cap 1, in-flight budget 6 and queue deadline 500ms held fixed at E2's sane-v1 values) at ~5x the E2-established capacity: accepted-request TTFT p95 degradation vs the SAME-config ~1x capacity-boundary baseline pulled under 20% (down from E2's measured +25.16%), because each accepted request now carries at most ONE queue-slot transit instead of up to three; shed rate at 5x is EXPECTED TO RISE relative to E2's sane-v1 (cap=3) 5x point (77.53% shed) because the shallower queue admits less of the offered burst before shedding -- this is the accepted trade, not a defect, and is reported next to every latency number. Sheds remain typed 503 overloaded + Retry-After (unchanged code path; only the cap values differ).
workload: Reuses E2's own workload files verbatim, unchanged: ib-t010-e2-baseline@1.0.0 (seed 10010202, rate 37.8072 rps, 350 req, admission-sane-v1b only) as the ~1x capacity-boundary reference, and ib-t010-e2-overload@1.0.0 (seed 10010201, rate 189.0362 rps, 900 req, admission-sane-v1b only) as the ~5x point. Reusing E2's exact seeds/rates/request-counts (rather than re-deriving from a fresh probe) is a deliberate single-variable-purity choice: it holds the offered load identical to E2's own baseline/5x-sane points so that the ONLY thing that differs between E2's sane-v1 numbers and E2b's sane-v1b numbers is the queue-cap change (3 -> 1), not a second re-probed rate that could drift for unrelated reasons. A fresh capacity probe is still run against admission-sane-v1b for box-quiet/structural parity with E2 and as a cross-check that capacity has not shifted (capacity is expected to remain budget-bound at ~37.8 rps since -admission-global-inflight-budget=6 is unchanged and queue cap does not gate steady-state throughput, only queueing depth) -- its result is recorded but NOT used to re-derive the measured-point rates.
constants: engine (mock-backend: same seed=43/ttft=80ms/itl=10ms/error-rate=0), model checkpoint (mock-8b), hardware (4-core CPU local-dev-container, no GPU), workload version+seed+rate+request-count (identical to E2, see 'workload' above), warm-up policy (50/rep excluded), gateway.version (infergate @ 6827d8c3d177464c17fae3b4dc6c2c475323333b, same pin as E2, built read-only via git archive), tenancy (-auth-mode=none, single implicit default tenant), -admission-global-inflight-budget (6, held fixed per the G5-verifier prescription), -admission-queue-deadline (500ms, held fixed per the G5-verifier prescription) all held fixed vs E2's sane-v1 arm. ONLY -admission-tenant-queue-cap and -admission-global-queue-cap move, together, from 3 (E2) to 1 (E2b) -- the prescription explicitly pairs these two flags as a single logical 'queue cap' variable and this hypothesis documents that pairing rather than varying them independently. cap=0 is a degenerate, out-of-scope floor (it sheds every request that cannot be dispatched immediately, i.e. zero queueing at all, which is a qualitatively different regime -- pure admit-or-shed with no queue) -- 1 is the declared floor for this experiment.
stop_condition: the admission-sane-v1b 5x point completes >= 3 repetitions and the admission-sane-v1b 1x baseline completes >= 3 repetitions against the pinned mock backend; or the target becomes unreachable (typed target_unreachable abort) or the schedule-slip watchdog fires. Per the G5-verifier prescription: if the <=20% criterion is ALSO not met here, this is recorded as the second REFUTED result for the same root cause and no further queue-cap value is tried in this task -- gate review pauses to the user.
repeat_policy: >= 3 runs per point, pooled percentiles across repetitions (never averaged), median +/- range across runs reported
slo_reference: docs/evidence/ib-t010/e2.slo.json (same SLO file as E2 -- both experiments share the same model-serving scope and the SLO's thresholds were derived from E2's own baseline maxima with headroom, which remain valid reference bounds for E2b's baseline since the workload is identical); the G5 degradation criterion itself (accepted-request TTFT p95 at 5x vs the SAME-config 1x baseline, <=20%) is computed directly from E2b's own two points' pooled percentile tables, exactly as in E2, not through the goodput ratio
provenance_notes: This hypothesis file is EXP-ib-t010-e2b-queue-cap, the pre-declared follow-up prescribed by the fresh-context G5 gate verifier after E2's honest REFUTED verdict (docs/evidence/ib-t010/benchmark-report-1.md Sec 1 and Sec 3; docs/tasks.md IB-T010 status note). G5 criteria (docs/tasks.md, 05-execution-roadmap.md): 'at ~5x capacity: accepted-request TTFT p95 degrades <=20%; sheds typed; no starvation' -- program target (provenance=assumed/program-target), same target as E2. The E2 capacity estimate this experiment reuses (37.80724254139287 rps, probe achieved 37.8072/200.0000 offered against admission-sane-v1) is measured (docs/evidence/ib-t010/e2-capacity-estimate-rps.txt, e2-probe.log) -- note the correct value is 37.807 rps; earlier prose in benchmark-report-1.md Sec 3 rounded this to '37.66 rps' by transcription error, corrected here and via a one-line erratum note added to that file (not a rewrite of its published numbers). Starvation scope is identical to E2: intra-tenant no-starvation under -auth-mode=none (one implicit tenant), NOT cross-tenant fairness (IG-T011, deferred, needs -auth-mode=db). This experiment does not repeat E2's admission-off-v1 control arm: the variable under test here is the queue-cap value itself (not admission on/off), and E2's own admission-off-v1 5x point (0% shed, p95 83.2ms, the no-queueing floor this experiment's mechanism argument references) remains the relevant off-control reference on file -- re-running it would add cost without adding information, since nothing about the off-config changed.
